decision_question: Should we invest in or develop a strategy around nuclear fusion energy, and if so, when and in which segments?

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nuclear fusion will achieve net energy gain in a commercial-scale demonstration by 2035.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Determines investment timing and technology risk.
needed_evidence: Public milestones from major projects (ITER, SPARC, etc.), peer-reviewed papers on plasma performance.

hypotheses.hypothesis: The levelized cost of electricity (LCOE) from fusion will be above $50/MWh by 2050, making it uncompetitive with solar and wind.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Affects market adoption and business case.
needed_evidence: LCOE estimates from fusion companies, academic studies, comparison with renewable LCOE trends.
search_queries: levelized cost of energy fusion vs solar

hypotheses.hypothesis: Private fusion funding will exceed $10 billion cumulative by 2027, driven by venture capital and corporate investments.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Indicates market confidence and resource availability.
needed_evidence: Fusion industry association funding reports, Crunchbase data, company press releases.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Regulatory frameworks for fusion will be established in at least three countries by 2030, enabling commercial deployment.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Reduces policy risk and clarifies market entry.
needed_evidence: Government policy documents, regulatory agency announcements, legislative progress.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fusion will first be commercialized in niche applications (e.g., industrial heat, marine propulsion) rather than grid electricity.
needed_evidence: Company roadmaps, pilot project announcements, market analysis reports.

hypotheses.hypothesis: The fusion supply chain will face critical bottlenecks in tritium breeding and high-temperature superconductors, delaying deployment.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Highlights investment risks in enabling technologies.
needed_evidence: Supply chain studies, material production capacity, tritium availability reports.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Public opinion and environmental opposition will not be a significant barrier to fusion deployment, unlike fission.
hypotheses.decision_relevance: Affects social license and project timelines.
needed_evidence: Public opinion surveys, media analysis, comparison with fission acceptance.

market_sizing_plan.sizing_question: How large is the credible opportunity for Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications, and which variables still need validation?

methods.formula: Total addressable market (TWh) = Global electricity demand (TWh) * % of demand addressable by fusion (based on grid integration constraints)
variables: Global electricity demand projections (IEA, EIA)
Grid capacity for baseload power
Fusion plant capacity factor assumptions
known_limitations: Assumes fusion can replace all baseload; ignores regional variations and policy constraints.

methods.formula: Market size (GW) = Number of fusion plants * Average plant capacity (GW)
variables: Number of plants based on announced projects and realistic deployment rates
Average plant capacity from company specifications
known_limitations: Relies on optimistic company announcements; many projects may not materialize.

methods.formula: Adopted capacity (GW) = Potential adopters (utilities) * Adoption rate * Average plant size
variables: Number of utilities interested in fusion
Adoption rate based on LCOE competitiveness and regulatory readiness
Average plant size
known_limitations: Adoption rate is highly uncertain; depends on unproven cost and reliability.

methods.formula: Value pool ($) = Market size (GWh) * Price ($/MWh) - Cost ($/MWh)
variables: Market size from top-down or bottom-up
Price from wholesale electricity markets or PPA prices
Cost from LCOE estimates
known_limitations: Price and cost are highly uncertain; value pool may be negative initially.

methods.formula: Maximum deployable capacity (GW) = Constrained by tritium availability (kg) / Tritium consumption per GW-year
variables: Tritium production capacity from fission reactors or fusion breeders
Tritium consumption rate per fusion plant
known_limitations: Tritium breeding technology not yet demonstrated; estimates are speculative.

market_sizing_plan.evidence_rule: Use public source values when available; keep missing variables as validation tasks.

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Quantify TAM/SAM before writing a growth claim.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications market size forecast data

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Cross-check revenue pools with physical or usage demand.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications demand volume official data
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications customer count users

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Estimate realistic conversion from demand to revenue.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications adoption rate penetration
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications price ARPU ASP

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Test whether the opportunity can earn attractive economics.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications cost CAPEX OPEX

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Check whether supply can serve demand.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications capacity project pipeline

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Validate capital formation and investor conviction.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications funding investment by company
Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications regulation policy approval

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Separate market growth from player-level advantage.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications market share competitive landscape

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Verify that adoption exists outside narrative claims.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications customer case order announcement

validation_data_needs.decision_use: Anchor the commercialization clock in dated evidence.
search_queries: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications milestone timeline commercialization

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion LCOE vs. Solar and Wind LCOE (2020-2050)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: How does fusion cost compare to renewables over time?
required_metrics: Fusion LCOE ($/MWh) - central estimate and range
comparison_set: Fusion vs. solar PV vs. onshore wind
preferred_sources: Lazard Levelized Cost of Energy Analysis
Fusion industry reports (e.g., Fusion Industry Association, company white papers)
search_queries: IEA LCOE projections 2024 solar wind fusion
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official IEA/IRENA/Lazard data for solar and wind. For fusion, use published estimates from credible sources (e.g., MIT, FIA). If only two endpoints exist, mark intermediate years as estimates using CAGR or public forecast benchmarks.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Establishes the cost competitiveness challenge for fusion against established renewables.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: The report argues that fusion will struggle to compete with solar and wind on cost. This exhibit visualizes the projected LCOE trajectories.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Fusion LCOE remains above $50/MWh through 2050, while solar and wind continue to decline, making fusion uncompetitive for grid electricity.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion Funding Timeline (2010-2024)
comparison_set: Private vs. public investment over time
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use FIA data as primary; cross-check with Crunchbase. If annual data not available, use cumulative and interpolate.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Shows the growth in private and public investment, indicating market confidence.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: The report claims private fusion funding is surging. This exhibit quantifies the trend.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Private fusion funding has grown rapidly, exceeding $5 billion cumulative by 2024, but remains small relative to other energy investments.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion Project Pipeline and Milestones
chart_data_needs.executive_question: What are the key upcoming milestones and their probabilities?
required_metrics: Milestone (e.g., first plasma, Q>1, net energy, grid connection)
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official project announcements. Mark delays explicitly. If probability estimates exist, include them.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Maps the path to commercialization, highlighting key dates and risks.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: The report argues fusion is not imminent. This exhibit shows the timeline of major projects and milestones.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Net energy gain is expected around 2035, but commercial electricity is unlikely before 2050.

chart_data_needs.title: Tritium Supply vs. Demand for Fusion (2030-2050)
required_metrics: Tritium supply (kg/year) from CANDU reactors and other sources
Tritium breeding rate (kg/year) if technology deployed
preferred_sources: Fusion fuel cycle studies (e.g., from ITER)
search_queries: tritium demand per fusion plant kg/year
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use IAEA data as primary. If breeding rates are uncertain, show as a range. Mark intermediate years as estimates.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Identifies a critical supply chain bottleneck that could delay deployment.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: The report highlights tritium breeding as a key challenge. This exhibit quantifies the potential supply gap.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Tritium supply from existing sources is insufficient; breeding technology must be demonstrated at scale to avoid a shortfall.

chart_data_needs.title: Utility Interest in Fusion: Survey Results
chart_data_needs.executive_question: How many utilities are actively planning for fusion?
required_metrics: Region: North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Rest of World
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use published survey results. If no recent survey, note the date and source.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Demonstrates early market demand from potential customers.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: The report claims niche applications will come first. This exhibit shows utility interest levels.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Only a small fraction of utilities have formal fusion plans, indicating early-stage market readiness.

chart_data_needs.title: Fusion LCOE Sensitivity to Key Variables
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Which variables most affect fusion cost competitiveness?
required_metrics: Variable: Capital cost ($/kW), Capacity factor (%), Fuel cost ($/MWh), Discount rate (%)
comparison_set: Variables and their impact on LCOE
search_queries: fusion LCOE sensitivity analysis capital cost
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use published sensitivity analyses. If not available, create a simple model based on public data and label as estimate.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Shows the impact of key assumptions on LCOE, highlighting uncertainty.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: The report acknowledges high uncertainty in LCOE. This exhibit visualizes sensitivity.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Capital cost and capacity factor are the most influential variables; small changes can make fusion competitive or not.

chart_data_needs.title: Regulatory Frameworks for Fusion by Country (2024-2030)
chart_data_needs.executive_question: Which countries are leading in fusion regulation?
required_metrics: Country: USA, UK, Japan, Canada, China, EU
Regulatory stage: None, Draft, Finalized, Enacted
Year of enactment or expected year
preferred_sources: US NRC fusion regulatory framework documents
chart_data_needs.data_quality_rule: Use official government publications. If stage is uncertain, mark as 'in development'.
chart_data_needs.narrative_role: Shows the policy landscape and readiness for commercial deployment.
chart_data_needs.pre_exhibit_context: The report argues regulatory frameworks are needed. This exhibit maps progress.
chart_data_needs.post_exhibit_takeaway: Only a few countries have established frameworks; most are still developing, creating uncertainty for investors.

storyline_plan.core_question: Should we invest in or develop a strategy around nuclear fusion energy, and if so, when and in which segments?
storyline_plan.central_thesis: The storyline should be led by the strongest public evidence, then translated into management choices.
storyline_plan.narrative_focus: Evidence is densest around: funding, timeline, adoption, technology
storyline_plan.structure_logic: Open with the executive decision, show source-backed data exhibits, then move through business implications, risks and action gates.
evidence_must_cover: To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

Highlights of the FY 2027 Request The FES FY 2027 Request of $755.3 million is a decrease of $50.406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

All 8 awardees are presently working toward presenting pre-conceptual designs and technology roadmaps of their FPP concepts within the first 18 months of the Milestone program—roughly late 2025 (18 months into the Milestone Program).

Department of Energy (DOE) today announced $107 million in funding for six projects in the Fusion Innovative Research Engine (FIRE) Collaboratives , and that several privately funded fusion companies have completed early critical-path science and technology (S&T) milestones in the Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program (“the Milestone Program”).

storyline_plan.charting_rule: Charts may use only evidence_ledger values, dates, source counts or same-unit comparable values tied to public URLs.
storyline_plan.exhibit_narrative_rule: Every exhibit must be embedded in the section argument: prose sets up the management question before the exhibit and interprets the implication after it; never stack exhibits without prose.
